conflicts.subject: CL-V Dual Power Domestics existence and location
conflicts.claim_a: Chat-confirmed frozen snapshot, storage mirror, final PDF and known checksum.
conflicts.claim_b: Codex master handoff omits CL-V; connected Drive search did not locate the named tree.
conflicts.governing_rule: NP-008: absence from one repository is not proof of nonexistence.
conflicts.required_resolution: Direct read-only disk scan and checksum verification.
conflicts.blocks: CL-V migration and collection registry finalization

conflicts.subject: Crisis Liturgies Volume VI
conflicts.claim_a: Numbering sequence might suggest a sixth volume.
conflicts.claim_b: No verified CL-VI collection, issue roster, PDF, repository or production state exists.
conflicts.governing_rule: L-015: disputed state must be reconciled, never inferred.
conflicts.required_resolution: Human designation only if a real collection is admitted.

conflicts.subject: CL-VII production state
conflicts.claim_a: Codex initiation record says no built collection or governing source was found.
conflicts.claim_b: Later chat evidence indicates an active foundational reset, builder enforcement and quarantined Issue 01 renders.
conflicts.required_resolution: Locate the later repository tree, handoff, renders and current human state record.

conflicts.subject: Pygmalion final production state
conflicts.claim_a: Chat and run-state claims: 48/48 accepted, 12/12 locked, unconditional archive lock, publication conformance complete.
conflicts.claim_b: Later explicit QC: RETURN FOR REVISION; 48-page shell structurally wrong; archive and publication withheld.
conflicts.governing_rule: Newest valid human-ratified evidence governs only after provenance is reconciled.
conflicts.required_resolution: Compare timestamps, human approvals, run-state JSON, archive lock, source package and latest QC files.
conflicts.blocks: Pygmalion pilot freeze and public replacement

conflicts.subject: Pygmalion editable source and dependency completeness
conflicts.claim_a: Drive and local project trees contain extensive production records.
conflicts.claim_b: Latest QC says native editable layout, linked assets, font inventory and source/citation register are missing or incomplete.
conflicts.governing_rule: Frozen specification and complete dependencies are mandatory.
conflicts.required_resolution: Read-only dependency inventory and exact missing-file list.

conflicts.subject: CL-IV frozen, print and public states
conflicts.claim_a: Exact governing qualification: FROZEN RELEASE SNAPSHOT COMPLETE — NOT PRINT-READY.
conflicts.claim_b: Records also report print PDF QC pass, release lock, public screen derivative and web publication work.
conflicts.governing_rule: State distinctions: frozen, print-ready, public derivative and publicly released are separate.
conflicts.required_resolution: Create one qualified state record mapping each derivative and approval.

conflicts.subject: CL-IV legacy image-gate records
conflicts.claim_a: Older prompt documents say image gate open for restart.
conflicts.claim_b: Later registers say proofs complete, gate closed and no image generation authorized.
conflicts.governing_rule: Higher/newer state records govern; stale prompts cannot control execution.
conflicts.required_resolution: Mark older restart prompts superseded in migration registry.

conflicts.subject: Four-page functional vocabulary
conflicts.claim_a: Canon v1.1 uses Artifact / Liturgical Hymn / Gradient Translation / Archive Commentary.
conflicts.claim_b: Archive and expansion profiles use other label sets.
conflicts.governing_rule: CCR-CL-003 remains draft; builder may not infer mapping.
conflicts.blocks: Universal schema lock and Pygmalion compilation

conflicts.subject: Image authorization model
conflicts.claim_a: Legacy Chachipti packet reinstates INTIFADA as builder gate.
conflicts.claim_b: CCR-CL-002 and CL-OPS-001 use CONCEPT_APPROVED and APPROVED_FOR_RENDER.

conflicts.subject: Chachipti institutional role
conflicts.claim_a: Original packet asserts broad constitutional and orchestrator authority.
conflicts.claim_b: CL-OPS-001 and CL-PROD-ARCH-001A define it as bounded compiler.
conflicts.required_resolution: Issue Chachipti v0.2 and mark original governance claims superseded.
conflicts.blocks: Use of original packet as runtime authority

conflicts.subject: CL-OPS-001 and CL-PROD-ARCH-001 relationship
conflicts.claim_a: CL-OPS-001 is locked/effective and defines role sequence.
conflicts.claim_b: CL-PROD-ARCH-001A later ratifies a broader architecture lock set with named patches.
conflicts.governing_rule: Same-level records must explicitly state relationship and supersession.
conflicts.required_resolution: Add mutual references; CL-OPS governs role execution, CL-PROD-ARCH governs broader system structure.

conflicts.subject: The Death of the Bit Boot Hymm status
conflicts.claim_a: Codex handoff and execution assets treat Boot Hymm as required throughout.
conflicts.claim_b: Notion archive control describes it as optional/recommended formal device.
conflicts.governing_rule: Builder may not infer unresolved page requirements.
conflicts.required_resolution: Founder decides required, optional or profile-dependent.
conflicts.blocks: Final volume specification and release profile

conflicts.subject: The Death of the Bit working proof versus accepted publication design
conflicts.claim_a: 120-page proof and phase build records pass as working assembly.
conflicts.claim_b: Founder later rejected generic editorial pages and required black-field/silver-plate architecture.
conflicts.governing_rule: Working proof is not final visual admission.
conflicts.required_resolution: Name release profile and rebuild editorial system.

conflicts.subject: Duplicate Drive registries and control files
conflicts.claim_a: Multiple Drive IDs contain identically titled artifact, layout and proof registers.
conflicts.claim_b: No authoritative duplicate relationship is recorded.
conflicts.governing_rule: One operational source; mirrors and duplicates must be explicit.
conflicts.required_resolution: Compare checksums/content and classify canonical copy versus mirror.

conflicts.subject: Legacy absolute paths versus one canonical root
conflicts.claim_b: Death Bit uses C:\Users\steve\My Drive\The Gradient\_codex_work; other volumes use numbered Drive roots.
conflicts.required_resolution: Read-only inventory, choose canonical root, copy/validate/register before retirement.

conflicts.subject: Web availability versus archive/publication authority
conflicts.claim_a: Screen PDFs and web assets may be reachable and return HTTP 200.
conflicts.claim_b: Archive admission and publication authorization records may be absent, qualified or later withheld.
conflicts.governing_rule: FROZEN_RELEASE, PUBLICATION_AUTHORIZED and PUBLICLY_RELEASED are separate states.
conflicts.required_resolution: Publication audit with manifest, approval and URL verification.
